name: Hex.pm API
what_it_does: Official package registry REST API for Elixir and Erlang packages (hex packages), enabling package search, metadata retrieval, publishing, and organization management.
use_cases: Searching for Elixir/Erlang packages by name, keyword, or organization
Fetching package metadata, version history, and download statistics
Automating hex package publishing in Elixir project CI/CD pipelines
Auditing dependencies for known vulnerabilities via hex.pm security advisories
Building private package repositories using the Hex private repository API
not_for: Projects where Mix dependency management is sufficient without programmatic API
best_when: An agent builds or manages Elixir/Erlang projects and needs to interact with the Hex ecosystem programmatically.
avoid_when: You're working with languages other than Elixir or Erlang.

auth.notes: API keys with permission scopes: read packages, publish packages, read profile, etc. Keys generated from hex.pm account. Anonymous access for public read operations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: All public packages: free. Private packages: Hex Organizations from $7/month
pricing.notes: Public registry completely free. Private repositories require paid Hex Organization. Comparable to npm's private package pricing model.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec is available improving discoverability. API documentation is clear for the core operations. Error responses are JSON with descriptive messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations are idempotent. Package publishing enforces unique versioning — publishing same version twice returns an error.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limits are per IP for unauthenticated requests — agents on shared IPs may hit limits faster
Package retirement is a soft operation — retired packages can still be downloaded
API returns tarball URLs not raw source — extraction required for code analysis
Hex namespace is global — no organization-based package namespacing in public registry

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Scoped API keys reduce blast radius. Hex package signing with public keys provides supply chain integrity. OpenAPI spec for audit. EU-based hosting.
